desc: Göreme is the heart of Cappadocia's surreal tuff landscape, where cave churches, underground cities, and sunrise balloon flights create one of Turkey's most distinctive travel experiences. Spring and autumn are the strongest all-round seasons for hiking and flight reliability, while winter brings quieter, photogenic conditions and summer delivers longer but hotter days.

todo.name: Göreme Open-Air Museum
todo.desc: A monastic complex of rock-cut churches and chapels with vivid Byzantine frescoes — the essential first stop for understanding Cappadocia's history.

todo.name: Sunrise hot-air balloon flight
todo.desc: One of the world's iconic balloon experiences — drifting above Love Valley, Red Valley and the fairy chimneys at dawn.

todo.name: Rose Valley & Red Valley hike
todo.desc: The classic Cappadocia walk through sculpted sandstone ridges, cave chapels and panoramic viewpoints. Best in early morning or late afternoon.

todo.name: Uçhisar Castle
todo.desc: The highest point in the region — a honeycombed rock citadel with sweeping views across Göreme and distant volcanic peaks.

todo.name: Derinkuyu or Kaymaklı Underground City
todo.desc: Remarkable subterranean settlements descending through multiple levels of tunnels, chambers and ventilation shafts.

todo.name: Paşabağ (Monks Valley)
todo.desc: Perhaps the best concentration of iconic mushroom-shaped fairy chimneys — superb for photography in low evening light.

todo.name: Çavuşin village & church
todo.desc: An atmospheric old settlement of cave dwellings and rock-cut churches, with fewer crowds than Göreme centre.

todo.name: Ihlara Valley day trip
todo.desc: A lush canyon carved by the Melendiz river, lined with hidden cave churches and frescoes — very different from Göreme's open valleys.

todo.name: ATV or horseback sunset tour
todo.desc: A fun way to cover multiple valleys quickly and catch golden-hour views over the rock formations.

todo.name: Turkish night & local pottery in Avanos
todo.desc: Combine regional Cappadocian dishes with Avanos' long pottery tradition on the Kızılırmak river.

wifi.note: Most cave hotels and central cafés are workable for remote tasks, though stone structures and valley layout can cause room-to-room signal variation.

prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; most UK routings connect via Istanbul to Nevşehir (NAV) or Kayseri (ASR). Return fares are often ~£180–£340 off-peak, ~£340–£520 in shoulder demand, and ~£520+ around spring/autumn peak windows.
prac.visa: Entry requirements vary by nationality and policy period; check Turkey's latest visa/e-visa rules before travel.
alerts: Balloon flights are weather-dependent — keep at least two dawn windows in your plan.
Popular cave hotels and flight slots sell out fast in April–June and September–October.
Early mornings can be cold even in warm months; pack layers for sunrise activities.

sweet: Late April to June and September to October are usually ideal for balloon conditions, hiking temperatures, and stable logistics.
